old_claim.note: Construction provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No monumental-construction dependency remains because architectural building practice is neither the experimental method nor mathematical mechanism of low-speed aerodynamic science.

source_shape.source_locator: NASA frames Cayley's achievement through aerodynamic forces, aircraft configuration, experiments, and quantitative flight analysis rather than monumental architecture.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies aerodynamic analysis and experimentation as the foundation of the scoped field.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The graph's construction node is Classical monumental architecture, so keeping it would launder generic making/building context into an unrelated scientific prerequisite.

ontology_before: Classical monumental construction was treated as an enabling dependency of aerodynamic science.
ontology_after: Low-speed aerodynamics depends on scientific method and mathematics, not an unrelated architecture node.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: construction is no longer a direct prerequisite for aerodynamics_subsonic.
would_reject_if: The construction node were respecified as aerodynamic model fabrication or wind-tunnel construction.
A source established monumental architectural construction as a direct method dependency of Cayley's aerodynamics.
short_reason: Monumental construction is not an aerodynamic-science prerequisite.
